steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $19 \left(x-12y\right) \left(x+12y\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-144y^2$ is a difference of squares because $144y^2=(12y)^2$. Use the pattern $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
$$x^2-144y^2=x^2-(12y)^2=(x-12y)(x+12y)$$
Keeping the outside factor of $19$ gives:
$$19(x^2-144y^2)=19(x-12y)(x+12y)$$
This is Choice D.
